programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: Two decades after a Jacksonville, Fla., woman goes missing, her son solves the mystery that haunted his childhood.

programme.title: Dateline NBC
programme.desc: A family begins a relentless pursuit of justice after the death of Nevada attorney Susan Winters is ruled a suicide; Susan's parents open up about the seven-year case and their efforts to push back against the criminal justice system.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a 6-year-old disappears, her father becomes the prime suspect; he fails a polygraph test and even confesses, but 10 years later, the investigation gets turned on its head.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a young woman disappeared, police feared she was the latest victim in a string of similar crimes. A pair of bloody gloves, unique tire tracks, and ordinary grass and pine needles provided investigators with some extraordinary clues.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two eyewitnesses may hold the key to the death of a motorcycle gang member.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1991, Maine resident Pearl Smith is missing after an argument with her husband Bill Bruns. But when an investigation turns up a blood trail that leads to the couple's basement, police are sure they'll find the victim's body.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: It was supposed to be a routine motorcade for the Queen of England. But on the way to Yosemite National Park, a car carrying three Secret Service agents collided with a car driven by a deputy from the local sheriff's office.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman is killed while walking her dogs in the woods. Since she had no enemies and her husband has an alibi, police assume it was a hunting accident...until a strange letter turns up allegedly written by the woman before her death.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Lives changed in the 20 years following an unsolved murder, and so did forensic science. In time, a high-powered microscope and DNA profiling revealed not only a clue no one had seen before but also the identity of the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After a day of fishing in a small, quiet village in Switzerland, a teenage boy did not return home as planned. The investigation revealed some important microscopic evidence in the water near where he was last seen. It was the only forensic evidence detectives had. But would it be enough for them to find him?

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Edward Honaker was convicted of rape, sodomy and aggravated sexual battery; he was sentenced to three life terms in prison. Honaker steadfastly maintained his innocence. After years in prison and writing countless letters, he finally found someone who believed him, and was willing to pay for DNA testing which could prove he was telling the truth.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After a man's body is discovered a few miles from his home, police trace the crime back to his wife.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1996, 16-month-old Anne Grace Gimmenstad became very ill, was rushed to the hospital and died within two weeks. Following extensive medical examinations, it was determined that Anna had been the victim of the deadly bacteria E.coli.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In the series premiere of Forensic Files, the longest running true crime series in television history, a Connecticut flight attendant went missing and was never seen again. Police suspected her husband was guilty of murder and they were able to prove it - even though they never found the woman's body.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: How did the stalker obtain the security system code for his victim's home? How did he steal her personal photographs? Police needed answers, and they found them in the most unlikely of places.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When Russ Stager, a popular gym teacher, is found dead of what appears to be an accidental gunshot wound, his family becomes suspicious. They think his wife may have planned his murder. When police find that her previous husband died in questionable circumstances, they re-examine the crime scene and uncover evidence that the death was no accident.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A two-man shrimp boat sank when a fast-moving, violent storm struck the Louisiana coast. The captain drowned, and the deckhand was rescued fourteen hours later. The deckhand was considered a hero until the captain's body was found.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1987, a human skull and some bones were discovered at a Boy Scout Camp near Farmington, Missouri. A facial reconstruction aided the investigators in this case. Once the victim was identified as Bun Chee Nyhuis, her husband Richard H. Nyhuis, was no longer just a person of interest.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: It's usually easy to determine how a criminal entered the crime scene. But in this case, it was far from clear. It looked like the killer vanished into thin air...and perhaps he had.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A Colorado hunter who'd been shot three times was the victim of a well planned crime. Investigators suspected two weapons and two killers. But analysis of the forensic evidence pointed them in a different direction--one much closer to home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a man is gunned down in his own garage, police discover that a wound sustained by the grieving widow may have been self-inflicted. They turn to science to help unravel a twisted tale of lust, greed and deception.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Armed with little else, police hoped the shoe impressions found at a Lansing, Michigan crime scene would put their investigation of Audrey Nichols' murder back on track.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Forensic computer analysis, blood spatter analysis and a painstaking investigation lead police to a lawyer husband Michael 'Mick' Fletcher with a motive to kill his wife, since he was having a sexual affair with judge Susan Chrzanowski.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Some of the refugees who sought asylum in the United States after World War II lied about the atrocities in which they'd participated. Years later, when a high-ranking religious figure was suspected of war crimes, there seemed to be no way to prove his guilt or innocence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man disappears one year to the day after beginning an affair with his friend's wife. 15 years later, with the help of a metal detector, police found evidence which would prove what had happened, and who was responsible.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1987, a human skull and some bones were discovered at a Boy Scout Camp near Farmington, Missouri. A facial reconstruction aided the investigators in this case. Once the victim was identified as Bun Chee Nyhuis, her husband Richard H. Nyhuis, was no longer just a person of interest.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When police recovered the submerged car of a man reported missing, they expected to find his body _ but it wasn't there. The investigation which followed would uncover an obsession turned deadly, and the motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An obstetrician returned home from the hospital and found his wife on the bathroom floor. She was covered with blood and not breathing. He tried unsuccessfully to revive her, staining his clothes with her blood in the process, and then he called 911.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On May 12, 1994, Crystal Perry was viciously murdered in her home in Bridgton, Maine. Michael Hutchinson, 32, was found guilty of murder in the 1994 slaying at Perry's home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A surgeon is stabbed to death, and the most likely suspect is seen in a restaurant at the time of the murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A young woman is found dead in her apartment. With little evidence at the scene, the case turns cold... until police discover a link between their prime suspect and an unsolved murder committed six years earlier in a different state.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The murders of an elderly couple leave police stumped until, two years later, they receive a major break. A jeweler looking through old newspaper clippings recognizes a necklace the female victim wore as having been pawned in her own shop.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A patrol officer is shocked by the amount of blood at a traffic accident and is shocked by the coroner's ruling until an anonymous phone call re-ignites the case.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An 11-year-old boy comes down with a fever and diarrhea after eating an undercooked burger; when his condition worsens, his mother drives him to a hospital and doctors discover E. coli destroying his body.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2002, a small community in upstate New York was devastated when Patty Wlasiuk died in a truck accident. But burdock seed pods found in her hair and on her clothes would indicate that this was no accident. It was cold-blooded murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: For 15 months, a serial killer was strangling prostitutes in Florida, then taunting police by leaving the bodies in plain sight. The only clues were a tire impression and some threads.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On May 20, 2001, Carrie Nelson, then 20, was beaten to death while working alone in a park office. The case went unsolved for six years until May 2007 when police found a match with DNA samples gathered from the crime scene.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1989, the dead bodies of Joan Rogers and her two teenage daughters were found floating in Tampa Bay.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: For seven years, a trio of men robbed one bank after another. They always got away before the police arrived, and they left no evidence behind. But the way they stood and the clothes they wore told a story that could be read by forensic scientists.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2000, Christina Sanoubane was murdered in her Cedar Rapids apartment after moving away from her abusive boyfriend. There are clues at the scene: bloody footprints and DNA from the victim's rape kit.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman was found dead on the bedroom floor of her apartment. The crime scene yielded little of value, and investigators wondered if they would find enough evidence to make a case, much less catch a killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: For six years, a serial killer prowled the streets of New York City. He wrote letters to police and The New York Post, indicating he would kill twelve people, one for each astrological sign.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2006, Darlene VanderGiesen received threatening emails and then disappeared. Tracking the source of the emails led police to the home of Daphne Wright, where they believe a murder was committed.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The bomb was constructed to cause as much damage as possible... and it did, killing the victim with deadly force and flame. A painstaking search yielded tiny clues, which identified the killer as surely as if he'd signed them.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The founder of American Atheists disappeared in 1995 along with one of her sons and granddaughter, along with $500,000 in gold coins. The police homed in on several suspects, including David R. Waters, who worked for American Atheists.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: How scientists solved a 1992 murder case involving cremation. It takes a long time and a very hot fire to cremate a human body, and thus destroy evidence of foul play. But the coroner who performed the autopsy on the body found telltale clues.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An examination of the first time animal DNA was used to solve a criminal case. When the body of Douglas Beamish's estranged wife Shirley Duguay was discovered, authorities linked 20 cat hairs found on her jacket to a cat owned by Beamish's parents.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After a street fight took the life of a national wrestling champion, police had to determine if he was killed in cold blood, or in self-defense. A jury decided it was murder, and sentenced the accused to a minimum of twenty years in prison.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a hit-and-run accident claimed the life of a high school athlete, everyone in town mourned his passing. Finding the killer was a long shot at best.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The medical examiner ruled the death an accident, but the detectives investigating the case thought the evidence at the scene indicated otherwise.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1971, John List left a note with the bodies of his mother, wife and three children in his mansion ballroom right before he disappeared. Eighteen years later, all detectives had to work from is an outdated photograph of List. "America's Most Wanted" commissioned an age-scaled bust of List to help viewers in identifying the confessed murderer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Some of the refugees who sought asylum in the United States after World War II lied about the atrocities in which they'd participated. Years later, when a high-ranking religious figure was suspected of war crimes, there seemed to be no way to prove his guilt or innocence.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The investigation into a brutal attack on two boys near a pond relies on evidence fished out of the murky water. It was the first case where Diatom evidence was used to place a suspect at the scene of the crime.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The driver said he couldn't have hit and killed a pedestrian on a Harrisburg street. The Jeep Grand Cherokee he was leasing around that time had been sold months ago to a buyer in another state.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The investigation into the apparent overdose suicide of Reverend Bill Guthrie's wife Sharon. Detectives suspect foul play after they recover incriminating files from Bill's computer and learn of his infidelity.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On the night of May 22, 1992, Betty Wilson returned home after a meeting. She walked up the stairs to the bedroom and discovered her husband, lying in a pool of blood. Jack Wilson had obviously been murdered... but how? And by whom? Even the experts couldn't agree.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The woman in the back of the truck was flailing her arms, screaming. They thought she was doing something dangerous for the fun of it. But when they found a jacket near a pool of blood, they knew what they'd seen wasn't a joy ride.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: No one in a quiet residential community saw anything unusual the day one of their neighbors was brutally attacked and murdered. Fingerprints found at the crime scene and surveillance video helped investigators to apprehend the presumed killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1971, John List left a note with the bodies of his mother, wife and three children in his mansion ballroom right before he disappeared. Eighteen years later, all detectives had to work from is an outdated photograph of List. "America's Most Wanted" commissioned an age-scaled bust of List to help viewers in identifying the confessed murderer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: How did the stalker obtain the security system code for his victim's home? How did he steal her personal photographs? Police needed answers, and they found them in the most unlikely of places.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1986, Edward Post left the Omni Hotel in St. Louis and went for a 40-minute jog. Before leaving, he said that he drew a bath for his wife Julie Thigpen-Post. Ed returned and called the front desk, stating that his wife had fallen in the bathtub and was not breathing and she was dead when emergency response arrived.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Two men were murdered while sleeping in their beds. The next night, arson destroyed a family planning clinic. Investigators wonder whether some shards of glass, paint chips and a chicken feather could link what appeared to be two separate crimes.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: While Earl Morris was vacationing in California, he learned his wife had gone missing from their home in Arizona. The search for Ruby Morris involved dozens of investigators and scientists, even the coast guard. And the results of that investigation surprised everyone... especially Earl Morris.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 19-year-old woman was found dead in her car; the scene had been staged to make murder appear to be suicide. During their investigation, police discovered the victim was involved in a love quadrangle, giving several people a motive to kill her.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The founder of American Atheists disappeared in 1995 along with one of her sons and granddaughter, along with $500,000 in gold coins. The police homed in on several suspects, including David R. Waters, who worked for American Atheists.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A serial killer was on the loose and police had to find him before he struck again. Their most promising lead was an unusual one: a bloody fingerprint on the body of one of the victims.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In this classic episode of Forensic Files, the longest running true crime series in television history, a serial bomber was waging a vendetta against the legal profession: A judge, two attorneys and even a courthouse had been targeted. Employing a range of forensic techniques, investigators used evidence from the bombs to lead them to the perpetrator.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A college senior was found raped and murdered near an unpaved footpath used by students to walk from one side of campus to the other.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1994, a human skull retrieved from an Ohio pond reveals a ghastly crime. Markings on the skull indicate that the victim had been stabbed multiple times and that the teeth had been removed with needle-nose pliers.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Susie Mowbray was charged for her husband Bill Mowbray's death. Her son was so convinced of her innocence that he enrolled in law school, studied all of the evidence and, eventually, discovered the truth of what really happened between his parents.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An examination of the 2002 DC area shooting spree that resulted in ten murders.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1996, 16-month-old Anne Grace Gimmenstad became very ill, was rushed to the hospital and died within two weeks. Following extensive medical examinations, it was determined that Anna had been the victim of the deadly bacteria E.coli.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: On May 12, 1994, Crystal Perry was viciously murdered in her home in Bridgton, Maine. Michael Hutchinson, 32, was found guilty of murder in the 1994 slaying at Perry's home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: After a man's body is discovered a few miles from his home, police trace the crime back to his wife.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2003, Kathy Lorick was murdered on a Concord, California jogging trail in the middle of the day while talking to her husband on her mobile phone.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Robert Sims returned home after working the night shift, and found his wife, Paula, unconscious on the kitchen floor. Their two-year-old son, Randy, was asleep in an upstairs bedroom, but their six-week-old daughter, Heather, was missing. Paula Sims was the only witness to a crime that baffles investigators to this very day.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: They thought the fire would cover their crime. But one tiny clue, no bigger than a thumbtack, remained. It held all the information investigators needed to put a trio of cold-blooded killers behind bars.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A college student was found dead, and the evidence suggested he knew his killer. Three hairs and some microscopic cells helped police to unravel a web of lies, and find the motive for murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When hunters found a skull in a Texas canyon, police found bits of clothing, small bones and a strand of hair. A forensic artist reconstructed her face. Now all they had to do was find her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When a woman went missing, friends and family were determined to find her. Their worst fears were confirmed weeks later when her body was discovered.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1995, when off-duty Maine State Trooper Vicky Gardner is attacked by Steven Fortin during a routine stop, it triggers a chain of events which jumpstarts a stalled murder case in New Jersey.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When an elderly couple died in a suspicious house fire, their son became the prime suspect. Investigators turned to forensic science to determine if the fire had been set deliberately, or if it was an unfortunate accident.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: For 15 months, a serial killer was strangling prostitutes in Florida, then taunting police by leaving the bodies in plain sight. The only clues were a tire impression and some threads.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The woman had been sexually assaulted, stabbed repeatedly, and left for dead. When someone who fit that description practically turned himself in, police were sure they had their man... until the DNA evidence proved them wrong.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 2004, nursing student Tamika Huston went missing from her Spartanburg, South Carolina home. A tip led detectives to her car and they find an unknown house key that could help solve the case.¾

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: When court clerk Peter Porco doesn't report to his work in November 2004, a courts officer is ordered to the Porcos' family home in Bethlehem, NY.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1995, Lisa Manderach and her daughter Devon left their Pennsylvania home to go shopping, but didn't return home. Lisa's husband Jimmy called police in a panic to report their disappearance and directed them to a nearby shopping center.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Emergency dispatch received a call from a man who said his girlfriend shot and killed herself. The autopsy revealed that the gunshot wound was not self-inflicted and the evidence on her body would give police a golden opportunity to catch her killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: The 3-year-old son of parents in Dripping Springs, Texas develops a respiratory condition. Then the father develops signs of Alzheimer's disease, and an investigator reveals that their ill health is due to a form of black mold in their modern home.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1987, a human skull and some bones were discovered at a Boy Scout Camp near Farmington, Missouri. A facial reconstruction aided the investigators in this case. Once the victim was identified as Bun Chee Nyhuis, her husband Richard H. Nyhuis, was no longer just a person of interest.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: California police had several suspects in the robbery and brutal murder of an elderly couple. Ten years later, a determined forensic scientist used material from a dentist's office to uncover ballistic evidence which had been overlooked.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: It was supposed to be a routine motorcade for the Queen of England. But on the way to Yosemite National Park, a car carrying three Secret Service agents collided with a car driven by a deputy from the local sheriff's office.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1991, San Francisco pornographer Artie Mitchell was murdered in his home. His brother and business partner Jim is found near the scene carrying a rifle and later confesses to shooting Artie. The question is whether the shooting was pre-meditated.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: In 1990, Shirley Andronowich's body was found one morning after being brutally murdered and mutilated in Winnipeg, Manitoba. Witnesses told police Shirley and her husband Ed were seen fighting in a bar the night before. Police first suspected Ed and even arrested him after he confessed to the murder. However, all forensic evidence proved that Ed had actually nothing to do with the murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A 13-year-old girl went missing from her Colorado home, and the only evidence¾the kidnapper¾left behind was three fingerprints on a window screen.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: An investigation into the murder of Katie Poirier is stymied by the lack of a body, but they find a few charred remains including a tooth. The tooth's filling contains unique properties that help investigators identify her remains. Further forensic evidence is then used to convict Donald Blom of her murder.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police are dispatched to the scene of a shooting, and discover the victim's husband is one of their own: a homicide detective who says his wife accidentally shot herself in the head.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Heavy rains and snow run-off carry manure into Milwaukee's water supply in 1993, leading to an epidemic of gastrointestinal illness affecting 400,000 people.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A man died in his home after a long history of heart disease. It was assumed that this was the cause of his death. A few weeks after the funeral, rumors surfaced that the death was no accident.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: Police instituted an intense search when the mother of two young children went missing. Twenty years after finding her body, advances in technology enable investigators to see evidence in a new light, and discover it pointed directly to the killer.

programme.title: Forensic Files
programme.desc: A woman disappeared and her husband became the prime suspect -- especially after police found a huge bloodstain on the carpet of their bedroom. When they sprayed the bedroom with Luminol, they discovered it was awash with blood spatter. Complex DNA testing proved it was the wife's blood. Now all they had to do was find her body.

programme.title: Payback
programme.desc: Back-to-back homicide cases send detectives down a twisted cycle of street violence as a connection between the two men’s deaths comes to light. As the investigation carries on, authorities must rely on social media to help them crack the case.

programme.title: Fatal Attraction-Dupe

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Midsomer University science fellow and cycling enthusiast George Jeffers (David Haig) threatens to make public a problem with his state-of-the-art invention Kernel Logic, but American software boss Clinton Finn fears he will lose millions if the truth comes out. When a schoolteacher is killed in a hit-and-run, Barnaby suspects Jeffers was the intended target. The subsequent murder of a fellow cyclist (Philip Jackson) brings the involvement of fellow University associates (Jonathan Cecil and Jim Norton) into sharper focus.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: In the peaceful village of Little Worthy, Bob Moss was about to open up his beloved model village, when he found a gruesome extra attraction. Young local, Richard Tanner, had been stabbed in the side and his corpse tied down like Gulliver in Lilliput. Barnaby and Jones wonder who wanted him dead, but when the woman he was secretly meeting is killed during the village's Crazy Craft race, the pair have to delve in and discover who might be lying and hiding secrets.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A cat burglar is on the loose, one known as "the Creeper", but the wave of burglaries he or she have committed, seem to have finally led to murder, when David Roper (Rik Mayall) is found smothered in his bed on the Chettham family estate. Yet Barnaby and Jones learn he may have been planning to write a book that could have been quite uncomfortable for someone, leading the pair to determine whether members of the Chettham family, or their friends, were responsible.

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A sleep-walking school teacher (Nancy Carroll) starts to believe she has slit the throats of her mentor (Tim Wylton) and a local handyman man in her sleep, but her friends (Paola Dionisotti and Monica Dolan) are not convinced that she is the culprit, nor is Barnaby. A local philanthropist (Paul Chapman) and his social-climbing wife (Suzanne Burden) are planning the annual Frobisher night - but will it go without a hitch, and can they identify the killer?

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: The residents of Milton Cross, a feudal estate with a church, farms, houses and acres of land, depend on lord of the manor Edward Milton for their livelihoods. But when Sonia Woodley is stabbed in the churchyard two years after the death of her abusive husband Gerald, Barnaby and Jones investigate, trying to work out who killed her. It soon becomes clear someone took a pair of tailor scissors to do the deed, but who amongst the villagers could have done so, is unclear, and the investigation is complicated, when the pair learn the local Anglican priest, the parish vicar, had received a letter from Sonia. When he himself is killed, the pair believe something in the letter may be key to solving the mystery.

programme.title: LEADERS with Francine Lacqua:Leading a Trillion-Dollar Company amid Crisis & Change
programme.desc: What drives the world’s most influential minds? In Leaders with Francine Lacqua, Bloomberg’s award-winning anchor sits down with the decision-makers shaping our future to explore the personal stories behind their public personas. Discover what inspires them, how they navigate power and the lessons they offer on defining success. Insightful, cinematic and elegantly crafted, Leaders goes beyond the headlines to reveal the human side of leadership.

programme.title: De lo que sea
programme.desc: Cinco profesionales desempleados, cansados de asistir a entrevistas laborales fallidas, deciden crear una empresa de servicios varios, pero lo que parecían ser trabajos fáciles, realmente llevan cierto grado de dificultad.

programme.title: El soplo de la muerte
programme.desc: El agente Murrieta de la policía judicial busca vengar la muerte de su familia mientras se enfrenta a un peligroso grupo de contrabando de diamantes.

programme.title: La Sorpresa
programme.desc: Dos hombres, Liborio y Tomás, descubren que aman a la misma mujer, Selena, y luchan por su amor hasta el duro final de su decisión.

programme.title: Amar
programme.desc: Laura y Carlos son dos jóvenes que se aman como si cada día fuera el último de sus vidas. Sin embargo, esa intensidad del primer amor será lo que les separará un año después.

programme.title: Moisés y los diez mandamientos
programme.desc: La historia de Moisés desde su nacimiento y llegada a manos de la princesa Henutmire hasta llevar a su pueblo a la Tierra Prometida. Su crianza como un verdadero príncipe egipcio junto a su tío Ramsés.

programme.desc: La compañía que maneja el grupo ídolo ANJELL insiste en contratar un nuevo cantante porque la voz del cantante principal Tae Kyung, ya no es la misma.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Mahinur le reclama a su hermano por pasar toda la noche fuera de casa. Melissa le dice a Metin que Öyku es muy linda. Melissa le confiesa a Görkem que Orhan trabaja con Más Orgánica, pero el chico le dice que es absurdo que piense eso.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Görkem le dice a su padre que la señora Mahinur se veía como si huyera de algo. Defne habla con su padre. Metin va a casa de Öyku y se quedan juntos hasta el amanecer. Mahinur va a la cafetería y escucha comentarios malintencionados sobre ella.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Manihur confronta a unas mujeres que hablan mal de su hermano en la cafetería. Melissa y Cuneyt llevan un rico pastel a casa de Manihur. Öyku y Metin chocan con Fevzi. Mustafá le avisa a Fevzi que han encontrado a la testigo.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Öyku recuerda su secuestro. Orhan humilla a Busra después de pasar la noche con ella. Manihur revela que entregó el anillo a Metin para que se lo dé a Öyku.

programme.title: Melissa
programme.desc: Öyku no puede dejar de pensar en Metin. Kadir lleva a Yesim a casa de su madre. Metin visita a Öyku, pero ella le pide que se marche. Metin conversa con Cuneyt y le confiesa que está enamorado.

programme.title: Señora del Destino
programme.desc: María do Carmo es una madre de cinco hijos que triunfó en la vida con mucha lucha y tesón, pero que todavía tendrá una batalla que luchar: obtener el cariño de su hija quien fue secuestrada cuando era una recién nacida.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Alicia, sobrina de la esposa de Albieri, entra a la oficina del científico y revisa todo. Halla la agenda donde él tiene el diario sobre la clonación de Lucas. Finalmente, Jade llega a Miami.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Lucas se siente cada vez más amargado y frustrado, nada le sale bien, su relación con Marisa es un desastre, su hija le grita y le reprocha sus defectos. Se desahoga con su padre Leonardo.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Jade está muy nerviosa por el encuentro que se dará entre Said y Lucas y para eso intenta poner sobre aviso a su ex, pero no lo logra. Cristina seduce a Leonardo, él vuelve a caer rendido.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Lucas ve a Cristina con la joya de Jade y la compra para devolverla a su dueña. Leonardo organiza una cena para Said y su familia; después de 12 años, Jade y Lucas vuelven a encontrarse.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: A Jade la delatan sus ojos de enamorada, Said se llena de ira y celos al observarla. Marisa no soporta la situación y humilla a Jade, mientras esta se imagina viviendo en la casa de su rival.

programme.title: La esclava Isaura
programme.desc: Una historia que se desenvuelve a mediados del siglo XIX en Brasil y relata la vida de Isaura, una esclava mestiza, hija de la esclava del Comendador y el capataz de la hacienda.

programme.desc: Öyku no puede dejar de pensar en Metin. Kadir lleva a Yesim a casa de su madre. Metin visita a Öyku, pero ella le pide que se marche. Metin conversa con Cuneyt y le confiesa que está enamorado.

programme.title: Destinos cruzados
programme.desc: Toprak despierta de un coma tras cinco años sin recordar nada de su pasado, y regresa a una familia que no reconoce, incluida su prometida de toda la vida y un niño que vive con él. Sin embargo, su amnesia lo mantiene en una incertidumbre constante.

programme.title: El clon
programme.desc: Llega el día de la cena en casa de Mohamed y Latifa, Jade y Lucas se miran fijamente; Said es testigo. Natalia sufre una desilusión muy grande y piensa evadirse del mundo mediante la droga.

programme.title: Eres mi estrella
programme.desc: La compañía que maneja el grupo ídolo ANJELL insiste en contratar un nuevo cantante porque la voz del cantante principal Tae Kyung, ya no es la misma.

programme.title: Génesis
programme.desc: Adaptación de uno de los libros más reconocidos de la Biblia, que aborda la historia del origen de la humanidad, invitando a entender el presente, volviendo al principio de todo. La caída de Lucifer, la vida de Adán y Eva, Noé y el diluvio, y otros.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Think you face challenges on the golf course? Tonight Nicole Estaphan meets a top-ranked young golfer, an amputee, who is working to advance the sport of adaptive golf and inspiring others along the way. Nicole brings us the story of a young family committed to supporting others while managing their own toddler’s severe illness – and his father’s battle with cancer. She also introduces us to a young man with cerebral palsy who is making his mark on the wrestling mat, and to two brothers who overcame challenging times as kids to chase their dream of running their own business.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Grief or tragedy can be difficult to explain. Some believe art can be a healthy way to express feelings that can’t be put into words. Tonight, Nicole Estaphan shares stories of people finding their voices through art including a young woman using dance to deal with her father’s past imprisonment; an artist teaching others to paint through the pain of grief; and music as therapy.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: the Chronicle team tonight!) but there’s universal agreement that apples, pumpkins, and cider are the flavors of fall. Tonight we get the story behind the spookily-named Phantom Farms in Cumberland, R.I., and raise a glass of Artifact hard cider. Prefer the smell of fall, rather than the taste? We meet a candle maker in Allston with a line of fall-scented candles. We also sample spirits inspired by cranberry bogs, and Anthony Everett learns the art of pumpkin-decorating from some jack-o-lantern experts.

programme.title: LIVE: CityLine
programme.desc: CityLine5 is WCVB's award-winning urban news and feature magazine talk show.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: “Hills, mills, and pork pies.” That’s how one local describes the city of Fall River. But as Erika Tarantal discovers, the Spindle City offers all that and so much more. Erika explores the next-generation market Portugalia, which highlights high-end goods from Portugal. If you think Fall River’s textile industry is a thing of the past, think again – Erika stops by factories taking advantage of the skilled local workforce to produce high-end linens and rugs. Erika meets teens finding community at the Watuppa Rowing Center. And she couldn’t leave town without checking out those pork pies! At Hartley’s, they’ve been using the same recipe for more than 100 years.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: A picture is worth a thousand words, and thanks to technology we all have the ability to capture powerful images. We visit the Griffin Museum of Photography in Winchester to discuss how that’s changing the landscape.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight, we are exploring the world of folk art, from the storied collection at Boston’s Museum of Fine Arts to collectibles crafted by hand in a Sutton studio. We find a treasure trove of historic weathervanes and consult the experts at Skinner on what’s hot. And are tattoos folk art? We’ll have the answer.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Try cuisines from all over the world.

programme.title: Wellness Hour: Restoring Your Smile
programme.desc: The Benefits Of Dental Implants.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Fun and fascinating foodie finds tonight (including one for Fido)! Erika Tarantal visits Quite Fetching in Grafton, a specialty bakery that makes special occasion cakes…for dogs. For hungry humans, we visit Nomai in Hingham, the new suburban venture from restaurateur Brian Moy. Ted Reinstein tours the headquarters of Boston Sword & Tuna in the Seaport district, and finds Boston’s working waterfront alive – and evolving. And Octoberfest may be six months away but we get a taste of a German beer hall at Trinktisch in Belmont.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Potato chips, Italian cold cuts, fresh bread, and smoked salmon. Dream menu? Nope, they’re just among the range of tasty stories we bring you tonight. In Gloucester, we meet a mother-daughter team getting buy-in from local consumers by selling them fresh local fish (and cooking it, too).

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: From family businesses to legacy brands and products - we are getting the stories behind them all.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Boston culinary icon Lydia Shire is still cooking, still creating – and still inspiring! Tonight she takes us from Chinatown to Cambridge, exploring the dishes and traditions that fuel her imagination—from roast duck to homemade lemongrass oil.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Great eats on the go. Nicole Estaphan hits the road with New England’s food trucks. Nicole introduces us to the local chefs and builders behind the scenes of these rolling kitchens.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: A spotlight on organizations focused on the Youth.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Beer without the alcohol, sweet treats without the gluten, and “mocktails” for everyone to enjoy – tonight, we begin with a hunt for treats “Free From” allergens and alcohol. Then, at an old-fashioned shop in Wakefield we find dairy-free ice cream with endless flavor options. In New Bedford we visit businesses focused on going waste-free (did you know your mattress can be recycled?). Finally, we meet a local skincare creator looking to keep your body, happy, with products free of artificial… anything!

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Food is expensive. Gas is expensive. Inflation is high, and enthusiasm for spending is down. So what can you do and see in Greater Boston – for FREE? We have ideas. Shayna Seymour shares the perks for anyone celebrating a birthday – free food! Anthony Everett takes a hike with a Boston man who maps walking trails and makes them available for (yes) free. We explore free public art all over Boston. And is your name Isabella? There’s a Boston museum where that will get you in for free.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Chronicle has gotten up close and personal with all manner of wildlife over the years. Charlotte the Bubble-Butt Turtle. Schubert the Seal. A trail-hiking rabbit. Baby bears. Tonight we get a little wild and reconnect with these furry friends.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Seafood in South Bay…a “salt cave” in Scituate…and a pickleball lesson for Shayna Seymour. Tonight it’s a sampling of food and fun fit for the weekend! We meet the couples behind The Pearl in Dorchester, check out Nautilus for good eats in the Seaport neighborhood, and join a running club (to work off the fried oysters, naturally). We also meet the creator of Boston-themed baby gifts at Joy Street Kids.

programme.desc: Nicole Estaphan steps aboard the floating lounge Tall Ship in East Boston for beverages and bites. (We’ll explain.) Those and other stories fit for a summer Friday!

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Are you ready to get a little bit spooky? We are getting in the mood for Halloween with a little history on how it got to where it is today. We walk you through some haunts in Salem, some well-known, and some not. Salem isn’t the only spot with ghostly ties; we get a tour of some of Boston’s haunted spots, including the Boston Common. Ted Reinstein takes a trip with paranormal investigators aboard an (allegedly) haunted ship in Mystic, Conn. And if you’re in the mood to experience your own Halloween thrills, we visit two local attractions still scaring visitors year after year.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: As temperatures drop, you may be hunting for indoor activities. Pac-Man and pinball enthusiasts, rejoice! In Norwood, a retro video game enthusiast has built his dream business, Hometown Arcade.

programme.title: Bringing Down The House: The Bombing of Harvey's Casino
programme.desc: In the summer of 1980, two men wheeled what looked like an IBM computer into a Lake Tahoe casino. The events that followed rocked the industry, baffled bomb technicians, and changed the lives of a criminal’s and a casino magnate’s family forever.

programme.title: The Lost Children of Carlisle
programme.desc: A powerful look at the history of Native American boarding schools as told through the stories of tribal members and their surviving family members, who endured an educational system founded on the concept “Kill the Indian in him, and save the man.”

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight we learn about the day jobs of local comedians, from New Hampshire’s Juston McKinney (a onetime sheriff’s deputy in Maine) to a Boston-area comic who helps families plan funerals, and another who is – believe it or not – a taxidermist.

programme.desc: The traditional workplace is changing and businesses and employees are changing with it. We also explore the child care challenge and hear from Massachusetts Gov. Charlie Baker about his administration’s Future of Work report.

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: Tonight, Shayna Seymour catches up with AJ Dybantsa. Also, Anthony Everett challenges an 8-year-old national chess champ to a match, while Erika Tarantal takes a lesson from the youngest-ever senior world champion in fencing!

programme.title: WCVB Chronicle
programme.desc: When two Northeastern University students felt excluded from an activity they loved they created a welcoming community of their own with Lonely Bones Skate Co., while pastry chef Gloria Cabral has won awards for her own talent but she is most proud of mentoring a new generation of pastry chefs. Tonight’s edition of “Gamechangers” focuses on people who are quietly making a difference right here in our community – from a Dorchester artist creating a new “face” for playing cards to a team making the sport of tennis accessible to all.

programme.title: The Best of Chronicle: Main Streets and Back Roads
programme.desc: It’s New Hampshire’s second-largest county, home to some of its best-known towns, and to famed Franconia Notch. Tonight Ted Reinstein shares the autumn beauty of Grafton County, N.H. He discovers how “The Notch” was once saved from development, and explores scenic views along the Cross-New Hampshire Adventure Trail. Hard to believe it’s been 20 years since the Old Man in the Mountain crumbled; Ted meets the team who designed “profilers” that help simulate the rocky view. And at Polly’s Pancake Parlor, Ted digs into a half-stack and catches up with old friends.

programme.desc: In the 1960s, Americans were dying at shocking rates due to inadequate emergency care. Enter Freedom House Ambulance Service: the first paramedics unit in the U.S., run and staffed by Black men and women. This is the origin story of EMS.
